mechanism_narrative: A persistently elevated federal funds rate above 5% raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and increases the relative attractiveness of short-duration fixed income, producing a persistent drag on S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon as long as the policy rate remains restrictive.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns is not significantly negative (|IC| < 0.03) over any 2-year rolling walk-forward window, or returns during high-rate periods do not underperform low-rate periods at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points from its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-like threshold), this signals an inflection into recessionary labor market deterioration, which historically precedes broad equity drawdowns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of latest snapshot, near multi-year lows
failure_condition: In more than 40% of historical instances where the 0.3pp threshold triggers, forward 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive, or the triggered-sample mean return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with elevated CPI inflation jointly signal an overheating economy that raises the probability of continued or renewed monetary tightening; the interaction of robust employment and sticky inflation drives negative equity risk sentiment over the next month because it delays anticipated rate cuts, whereas either signal alone is insufficient to move markets.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 1.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 158,638k and CPIAUCSL at 313.049 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with resilient labor market
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following joint-trigger episodes are not significantly lower than returns following single-signal or no-signal episodes (difference-in-means p > 0.10), or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts during a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS above 4.5%) signals interest-rate-sensitive demand destruction that spreads to broader consumer and construction-linked sectors, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon; the same housing decline in an easing regime instead reflects healthy rate transmission and is not bearish.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% from trailing 6-month peak while FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1277k units and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: The direction of the HOUST-return relationship does not flip sign between tightening and easing regimes (tested via regime-split regression), or the tightening-regime triggered sample shows non-negative mean forward 60-day return at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending, which is the largest component of GDP and a leading indicator of near-term corporate revenue; positive RSAFS growth momentum is associated with continued short-term equity market strength over the next trading week regardless of macro regime.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 703,088 million dollars as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward 5-day returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the sign of the correlation flips across more than one 2-year subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals contraction in manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which precedes broader economic slowdown fears and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as earnings outlooks for cyclical sectors deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.1168 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Following INDPRO decline triggers of this magnitude, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or mean triggered return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint state of decelerating real GDP growth alongside a still-elevated federal funds rate indicates monetary policy remaining tighter than the real economy can sustain, creating rising recession risk priced into equities over a quarterly horizon; neither slowing growth nor a high rate alone is sufficient because a high rate with strong growth reflects a soft landing rather than restrictive drag.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1.5% AND FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,758.752 billion and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with slowing growth
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns during joint-trigger periods are not significantly lower than returns during periods where only one condition holds (p > 0.10), or Rank IC of the joint interaction falls below 0.03 across any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a disinflationary regime where CPI growth is falling toward target while the federal funds rate remains high, markets anticipate imminent rate cuts, producing a short-term positive drift in equities over the next trading week as forward-looking discount rate expectations ease ahead of actual policy action; this effect is regime-specific and does not hold when inflation is reaccelerating.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL YoY moderating toward 3% with FEDFUNDS held at 5.33% as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: disinflationary pause at peak policy rate
failure_condition: During periods of confirmed disinflation with high FEDFUNDS, forward 5-day returns show no significant positive drift (mean return not significantly greater than unconditional mean at p > 0.10), or the effect disappears when re-tested on a held-out 2-year window.
